channel.title: Double Vision USA - Field Notes
channel.description: Guides for small shops, contractors, and operators.

item.title: CNC speeds and feeds calculator (SFM, RPM, IPM): formulas + ShopCalc
item.description: Feeds and speeds reference for mill and lathe: surface speed to RPM, chip load to feed rate, MRR, and starting SFM ranges. Same math as the ShopCalc app, free in the browser.

item.title: Tap drill chart and how to read thread sizes (UNC, UNF, M-series)
item.description: 75% and 60% thread engagement charts in inch and metric, the formula behind them, and when to drop to 60% on stainless and titanium.

item.title: NEC wire sizing for branch circuits (ampacity + voltage drop)
item.description: Pick the right copper or aluminum wire size with NEC 310.16, derate for ambient and conductor count, and check voltage drop on long runs.

item.title: MIG welding amperage and wire feed cheat sheet (carbon steel)
item.description: Starting points for amperage, voltage, wire feed speed, and gas selection by material thickness - plus how to read the puddle when you're outside the chart.

item.title: How much concrete do I need? Slab, footing, and pier yardage
item.description: The three formulas, overage rules of thumb, mix design and rebar references, and how to avoid the most common short-load and over-order mistakes.

item.title: Deck joist span and spacing (16" vs 12" oc, plus the Simpson SDWS that replaced 3/8" lag bolts)
item.description: AWC DCA 6 + IRC R507 spans for SPF and SYP, when composite forces 12" oc, the modern structural-screw replacement for ledger lags, and the joist-tape that adds a decade of life for $30.

item.title: How much drywall do I need? Sheets, screws, mud, and the paper-vs-mesh rule pros never break
item.description: Sheet-count formula, screw spacing, mud-and-tape estimating, and the brand-specific picks (USG green-lid vs blue-lid, Easy Sand 90, FibaFuse) - plus the IRC R302.6 5/8" Type X spec for garage ceilings.

item.title: How much paint do I need? The math, the brand picks pros buy, and the Floetrol trick
item.description: Coverage formula with realistic numbers, the one-coat tier (Aura, Emerald, Marquee), the primer decision tree, and the additive + brush picks (Floetrol, Wooster Shortcut, Purdy XL Glide) that decide whether the wall looks DIY or pro.

item.title: Schluter Ditra vs cement backer board for tile floors - when each one wins
item.description: How uncoupling membrane and rigid backer solve different problems, the substrate decision tree, ANSI A108.5 95% mortar coverage, the modified-vs-unmodified thinset rule, and where backer board still beats Ditra.

item.title: Drywall anchors and the 25-pound rule (plastic, toggle, SnapToggle, or just hit a stud)
item.description: Real-world load ratings for plastic, threaded, molly, and toggle anchors. When to use what, the SnapToggle install step everyone gets wrong, and why a TV mount never goes into drywall alone.

item.title: What speeds and feeds for a 1/4" 4-flute carbide endmill in 4140 steel?
item.description: Starting recipe: 280 SFM, 4,280 RPM, ~26 IPM, 0.0015 in/tooth - plus the SFM ranges for annealed/prehard/hardened 4140, the slot/profile/trochoidal adjustments, and what the spindle is telling you when it complains.

item.title: Tap drill for 1/2-13 UNC at 75% thread engagement (with the math)
item.description: Calculated tap drill is 0.4251". Closest commercial drill is 27/64" (~78% engagement). Drop to 7/16" for 60% on stainless and titanium. Full chart, formula, and the cutting-vs-roll-tap drill rule.

item.title: How to write G-code to face a 2.5" Ø part on a CNC lathe
item.description: Complete paste-able program for facing 0.030" off a 2.5" diameter bar in 4140 prehard, plus the math behind G50/G96, feed/speed, and the safe-retract sequence that keeps the insert away from the chuck.

item.title: How many tiles for a 12 × 14 ft bathroom?
item.description: 168 sq ft floor + 10% waste = 185 sq ft to order. In 12×24 tile that's 93 tiles or 12 boxes. Plus the tile-setter waste-factor rules, grout coverage, bullnose math, and why dye-lot mismatch ruins the floor.

item.title: How many bags of concrete for a 4-inch slab 20 × 15 ft?
item.description: 100 ft³ / 3.7 cu yd. That's ~225 60-lb bags or ~167 80-lb bags - but at this size you should call a ready-mix truck. Full math, bag-vs-truck threshold, and rebar/mesh recommendations.

item.title: How many sheets of drywall for a 12 × 14 ft room with a 9-foot ceiling?
item.description: 600 sq ft net area. 17 sheets of 4×9 (best fit), 19 of 4×8, 13 of 4×12. Why 9 ft ceilings change the math, plus screws, tape, mud, and the hang sequence that kills horizontal seams.

item.title: What amperage for 1/4" mild steel MIG with .035 ER70S-6?
item.description: 165–185 A at 19–21 V, ~290 IPM wire feed, 14–18 IPM travel, C25 gas at 35–45 CFH. The math behind those numbers, the puddle-reading symptoms, and when to switch from short-circuit to spray.

item.title: Heat input limit for SA-516 Gr 70 root pass
item.description: GTAW root: 10–25 kJ/in. SMAW root: 12–35 kJ/in. The real limit is the qualified WPS - ASME Section IX QW-409.1 makes heat input an essential variable. How HI affects HAZ toughness on notch-critical pressure-vessel steel.

item.title: TIG tungsten size for 180 A on stainless steel
item.description: 3/32" (2.4 mm) tungsten, 2% lanthanated or ceriated (skip the thoriated). Sharp 30° point with 0.005" flat. Plus the cup size, gas flow, filler rod, and color-check guide for clean stainless TIG.

item.title: What wire size for 40 A continuous, 180 ft, 240 V single-phase?
item.description: Ampacity says #8 Cu, voltage drop forces #6 Cu. Aluminum equivalent: #2. Full NEC 310.16 + 210.19 calculation, the EV-charger case (where this comes up most), and the conduit-fill check.

item.title: Conduit fill for 4× #10 AWG + 1× #6 in 3/4" EMT
item.description: Fill is 0.135 sq in in a conduit with 0.213 sq in (40%) capacity - 25.3% fill, comfortably legal under NEC Chapter 9. Plus the full table, alternative conduit types, and the 360° bend rule.

item.title: Voltage drop and NEC 210.19(A) explained
item.description: The 3% / 5% rule is in an Informational Note - recommendation, not enforceable code. Where it stops being optional, the formula, the math at the most-common run lengths, and how loads degrade with VD.

item.title: Write and send a professional quote (fast)
item.description: A practical structure plus a copy/paste template you can ship today. Get approvals faster without sounding stiff.

item.title: Bill of sale guide (template included)
item.description: What to include, when to use it, and a clean template for high-ticket items, equipment, and Marketplace deals.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace bill of sale (free) + safest way to get paid
item.description: Bill of sale template for Marketplace, how to sell safely in person, safest payment (cash, Zelle, ACH), scam red flags, and a clean handover checklist for tools, vehicles, and higher-ticket items.
